page: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton3 processor family, specifically the Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a 30 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. Multi-core stress-ng tests and Geekbench multi-core scores rank in the strong and top-tier categories respectively, aided by the dedicated single-thread-per-core design. Memory performance is highly efficient, with Passmark memory scores and large-block read/write bandwidths reaching top-tier and strong classifications. However, single-core performance metrics, such as single-threaded gzip compression and single-core stress-ng tests, remain in the average tier.

The server is highly optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations. It achieves top-tier results in Clang software compilation and Passmark database operations, alongside strong throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. While it handles medium-sized language model inference with reasonable text generation speeds, it is less efficient for smaller model text generation. This bare-metal instance is best suited for high-density multi-threaded applications, CI/CD pipelines, database hosting, and web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton3 Neoverse-V1 processors running at 2.6 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation speeds, and database operations, as evidenced by strong Geekbench and Passmark benchmarks. Conversely, single-core processing and small-model text generation benchmarks fall into average and poor categories, highlighting a tradeoff that favors highly parallelized workloads. The c7g.metal is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, multi-threaded applications, making it an optimal fit for software compilation, high-throughput database operations, web serving, and containerized microservices.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated ARM64 cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 dedicated ARM64 cores and 128 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare-metal ARM64 platform delivering high-density multi-core performance and DDR5 memory bandwidth for parallelized compute workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core performance and software compilation speeds
Strong throughput for database operations and static web serving
Optimized for CI/CD pipelines, container hosting, and parallel workloads
